genre_refs: Make America Great Again slogan riff
description: A designed slogan-and-headline-collage graphic. The top half is a photograph taken in an industrial/utility training yard: a young man in a long-sleeve tan shirt and a white 'Georgia Power' hard hat with sunglasses stands at left in profile, talking with a woman at right who wears a light gray blazer, sunglasses and a white 'Georgia Power' hard hat (numbered '29'); a third worker and white utility bucket trucks are visible behind them. The image fades into a dark navy lower section. Across the center in large serif type is the slogan 'Make America' (white italic) over 'SKILLED AGAIN!' (bold white caps) — a riff on the 'Make America Great Again' slogan. Below are three torn-paper-style clipped local-news headlines with outlet logos: a KSTP.com (5 Eyewitness News) clip 'Growing enrollment at trade schools in Minnesota'; a WGEM clip 'John Wood Community College sees increase in trade program enrollment'; and a Denver7 (ABC) clip 'Colorado trade schools see increased enrollment as students prioritize cheaper pathways to jobs'. The technique combines an aspirational workforce image, a MAGA-style slogan, and aggregated favorable headlines to promote the administration's 'skilled trades boom' narrative. Target audience: working-age Americans, especially young people considering the trades.
notable_text: Make America SKILLED AGAIN!; Growing enrollment at trade schools in Minnesota; John Wood Community College sees increase in trade program enrollment; Colorado trade schools see increased enrollment
